_comment: D178 Group 4 bounded under-told-perspectives system. This is a War Effort teaching/system slice, not a Soldier's Story replacement lane, not a new playable battle, and not a tokenized identity bonus. Women in the war remains the separate D153 lane. Default bridge input is exact zero; the optional perspective-liaison priority is capped, costly, and cannot write battle outputs.

US.name: Union armies of many peoples
US.summary: The Union war depended on enslaved people's self-emancipation, Black enlistment, immigrant regiments, German-American political mobilization, and Native and borderland service as well as the familiar generals.
US.teaching: The point is agency, not a diversity checklist: people forced policy, filled ranks, guided armies, carried news, and then fought over how their service would be remembered.
sources: data/manpower-teaching.json mp-immigrants-usct card, citing Lonn, McPherson, Glatthaar, Manning, Cornish, and official USCT records.
data/codex.json entries for Contraband of War, United States Colored Troops, Harriet Tubman, and Irish Brigade.

CS.name: Confederate contradictions and borderlands
CS.summary: The Confederate project relied on enslaved labor while fearing enslaved resistance, drew on Native and borderland alliances, and then fought memory battles that often erased coerced labor and divided loyalties.
CS.teaching: This surface does not treat Confederate diversity as exoneration. It names coerced labor, sovereignty pressures, elite policy, and divided communities without turning them into Lost Cause cover.
sources: data/decisions.json emancipation and twenty-slave-law cards, citing Du Bois, Foner, Oakes, McCurry, National Archives, and American Battlefield Trust.
HISTORICAL-DATA.md anti-Lost-Cause guardrails and Chickamauga note on Cherokee-origin place memory.

threads.label: Enslaved people's agency
threads.summary: Self-emancipation, contraband camps, labor withdrawal, Black scouting and guiding, and USCT enlistment made freedom a military fact before it became settled law.
threads.gameHook: Shows why emancipation, USCT enlistment, hard war, manpower, and prisoner policy are connected instead of separate moral sidebars.
sources: data/decisions.json emancipation-proclamation card, citing Du Bois, Black Reconstruction; National Archives; Library of Congress; American Battlefield Trust; Foner; and Oakes.
data/codex.json contraband-of-war and united-states-colored-troops entries, citing NPS, National Archives, Goodheart, and McPherson.

threads.label: Immigrant and ethnic units
threads.summary: Irish, German, and other immigrant soldiers were not a footnote: roughly a quarter of Union soldiers were foreign-born, and units such as the Irish Brigade and German-American XI Corps carried both service and scapegoating.
threads.gameHook: Connects manpower, command politics, named units, Chancellorsville's XI Corps blame, the Irish Brigade, and the German-American vote behind Sigel.
sources: data/manpower-teaching.json mp-immigrants-usct card and audit, citing Ella Lonn's foreign-born estimates and later manpower scholarship.
data/codex.json Irish Brigade entry and data/chancellorsville.json XI Corps teaching card, citing American Battlefield Trust, Susannah Ural, NPS, Sears, and Keller.

threads.label: Native nations and borderlands
threads.summary: Native nations and Native individuals faced a war inside older sovereignty crises: divided loyalties, Indian Territory fighting, Union service, Confederate alliances, and postwar memory that usually pushed them to the edge.
threads.gameHook: Keeps Native nations visible now without building a Trans-Mississippi playable battle or inventing unsupported OOB claims.
sources: Local source scan found Chickamauga Cherokee-place-name caution, D40/D54 Trans-Mississippi deferral, and general anti-Lost-Cause guardrails but no full Native-nations dossier yet.
Pending source pass should verify Ely S. Parker, Stand Watie, Cherokee division, Indian Territory fighting, and Honey Springs / Trans-Mississippi context before upgrading.
threads.sourceNote: Native-nations detail is intentionally narrow in this slice because the local repo had thinner Native source trails than the other threads. It is enough to keep the thread visible; it is not enough to create a playable Trans-Mississippi battle.

threads.label: Women in the war
threads.summary: D153 already built the women-in-war presentation lane for soldiers in disguise, relief, nursing, scouting, and disputed claims. This system cross-references that lane instead of copying it into Soldier's Story replacements.
threads.gameHook: Preserves D153 separation: no replacePid, no ss: ids, no registry combatant row, no unsupported portrait claim.
sources: data/women-in-war.json cw_women_in_war_v1 records: 7 records, 6 Verified and 1 Disputed, with D153 lane constraints.
tools/import-women-in-war.mjs and tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs gate the separate presentation lane.

debates.title: Agency, not tokenization
debates.summary: A perspective thread is not a collectible bonus. The game should show people acting in history, with costs and contradictions, rather than awarding identity-flavored buffs.
sources: AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md D34.1: all four under-told perspective threads, woven in and sourced, not tokenized.
V1-CHECKLIST.md Group 4 contract for bounded, anti-Lost-Cause War Effort systems.

debates.title: Black agency vs great-men-only history
debates.summary: Lincoln's policy mattered, but enslaved people forced the policy field by fleeing, laboring, guiding armies, and enlisting; a president-only story is incomplete.
sources: data/decisions.json emancipation-proclamation card, including Du Bois, Foner, Oakes, National Archives, and Library of Congress source trail.
data/codex.json contraband-of-war and Harriet Tubman entries.

debates.title: Service and scapegoating
debates.summary: Immigrant soldiers filled regiments and voting blocs, yet defeats could be blamed on them as outsiders, as with the German-American XI Corps after Chancellorsville.
sources: data/chancellorsville.json cv_xi_corps teaching card.
data/manpower-teaching.json mp-immigrants-usct card and data/codex.json Irish Brigade entry.

debates.title: Native nations need a later full pass
debates.summary: A narrow Native-nations thread is honest now; a full Trans-Mississippi / Indian Territory system needs its own sourced pass and should not be improvised inside this small War Effort slice.
sources: V1-CHECKLIST.md Group 7 keeps later battle-build work last and Phase G Trans-Mississippi deferred.
Current local source scan showed limited Native-nations coverage compared with women, USCT, immigrant, and hard-war trails.
